title: Benchmarking and Reproducibility

what: Stable files from the full wrapper
title: Benchmarking and Reproducibility
text: run_expranno() writes reproducibility-oriented side files default. wrapper now keeps: annotation_report.csv annotation_ambiguity.csv annotation_provenance.csv annotation_benchmark_summary.csv annotation_benchmark_coverage.csv annotation_validation_summary.csv annotation_validation_detail.csv session_info.txt files make easier re-run workflow later explain annotation sources active given result directory. reproducible machine-level setup, expranno also ships: inst/scripts/install_optional_backends.R inst/docker/Dockerfile
code: outdir <- tempfile("expranno-benchmark-") dir.create(outdir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  result <- run_expranno(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none",   output_dir = outdir,   run_deconvolution = FALSE,   run_signature = FALSE,   run_benchmark = TRUE,   benchmark_engines = c("none"),   verbose = FALSE )  basename(result$files) #> [1] "annotation_ambiguity.csv"          "annotation_benchmark_coverage.csv" #> [3] "annotation_benchmark_summary.csv"  "annotation_provenance.csv"         #> [5] "annotation_report.csv"             "expr_anno.csv"                     #> [7] "expr_meta_merged.csv"              "session_info.txt"

title: Getting Started with expranno
text: expranno exposes five annotation modes: "hybrid": recommended production mode "biomart": use biomaRt "orgdb": use org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db "ensdb": use EnsDb "none": skip annotation lookup keep normalized IDs "hybrid" default coverage-oriented mode. uses cascade: biomaRt fixed Ensembl release (v102 default) species-specific orgdb species-specific EnsDb mirrors practical manual workflow: query Ensembl first, fill unmapped IDs org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db, use EnsDb enrich structural fields biotype coordinates. lightweight docs tests, examples use "none". real runs, reproducible preset often easier share long list arguments. Examples: annotation_preset = "human_tpm_v102" annotation_preset = "mouse_tpm_v102" annotation_preset = "human_count_v102" annotation_preset = "mouse_count_v102" preset table quickest way compare recommended input scale, backend order, bundled truth resource. --one wrapper run_expranno(). reproducible production-style run, switch preset explicitly: run, can also return Bioconductor-native container. data already live SummarizedExperiment, can start directly.
code: annotated <- annotate_expr(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none" )  merged <- merge_expr_meta(   expr_anno = annotated$expr_anno,   meta = annotated$meta_checked ) result <- run_expranno(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none",   output_dir = tempdir(),   run_deconvolution = FALSE,   run_signature = FALSE )  result #> <expranno_result> #>   annotated genes: 3 #>   merged rows: 9 #>   deconvolution runs: 0 #>   signature runs: 0 #>   benchmark runs: 0 #>   validation runs: 0 run_expranno(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   annotation_preset = "human_count_v102",   output_dir = "results" ) annotated <- annotate_expr(   expr = demo$expr,   meta = demo$meta,   species = "human",   annotation_engine = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  if (requireNamespace("SummarizedExperiment", quietly = TRUE)) {   se <- as_expranno_se(annotated)   se } else {   "Install SummarizedExperiment to use as_expranno_se()." } #> class: SummarizedExperiment  #> dim: 3 3  #> metadata(1): expranno #> assays(1): expression #> rownames(3): ENSG00000141510 ENSG00000146648 ENSG00000012048 #> rowData names(48): gene_id_raw gene_id ... annotation_backend_mirror #>   annotation_date #> colnames(3): sample_a sample_b sample_c #> colData names(4): sample group batch species result <- run_expranno(   expr = se,   species = "human",   assay_name = "counts",   gene_id_col = "gene_id",   annotation_engine = "hybrid" )

title: Theory and Design
text: package aims six things well: enforce simple strict input contract maximize annotation coverage human mouse expose provenance ambiguity rather hiding support fixed presets repeated human mouse workflows keep expression metadata aligned make downstream immune signature analyses reproducible

what: Why the package starts with annotation
title: Theory and Design
text: Many downstream tools expect gene symbols rather Ensembl IDs. reliable annotation layer makes possible : keep original gene_id recover display-ready symbol attach gene-level metadata name biotype create symbol-based matrices deconvolution scoring

title: Theory and Design
text: default design coverage-first cascade: biomaRt fixed Ensembl release (v102 default) org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db optional EnsDb packages stage fills missing fields left previous stage. expranno can often recover usable annotation single-source lookup. also makes package behavior much closer careful manual annotation workflow. user can reason : ask Ensembl first fill missing symbols names OrgDb fill structural fields EnsDb

title: Theory and Design

title: Theory and Design
text: recommended default. designed maximize annotation coverage filling missing fields progressively across multiple sources. Human: biomaRt org.Hs.eg.db optional EnsDb.Hsapiens.v86 Mouse: biomaRt org.Mm.eg.db optional EnsDb.Mmusculus.v79 Use goal highest practical annotation rate. expranno also records source supplied chosen field stores unioned candidates ambiguity review.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses biomaRt. convenient want one consistent Ensembl-oriented source, expranno tied fixed Ensembl release default better reproducibility.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses org.Hs.eg.db org.Mm.eg.db. useful stable symbol identifier mapping, aim richest single-source annotation path.

title: Theory and Design
text: uses EnsDb. especially useful coordinate biotype-oriented annotation fields.

title: Theory and Design
text: skips external lookup keeps normalized Ensembl IDs. mainly useful tests, examples, lightweight documentation builds.

title: Theory and Design
text: Use "hybrid" real analysis. Use "biomart" want single online Ensembl source. Use "orgdb" need local annotation mapping layer. Use "ensdb" gene model fields main target. Use "none" dry runs, docs, testing package flow without external dependencies.

what: Why annotation coverage is a first-class output
title: Theory and Design
text: expranno treat annotation hidden preprocessing step. coverage report part workflow answers important downstream question immediately: annotation complete enough symbol-based analyses? practice, coverage report checked running Deconvolution Signature analysis. Strong symbol gene_name coverage usually indicates expression matrix ready steps, weak coverage often sign species backend configuration needs attention. package now writes three annotation-side files coverage alone enough: annotation_report.csv per-field coverage annotation_ambiguity.csv multi-candidate mappings annotation_provenance.csv backend release, package, run-date tracking Together files answer: much annotation recovered? come ? rows still need human review? repeated lab workflows, package now also ships built-presets human_tpm_v102 mouse_tpm_v102. meant make species, Ensembl release, version stripping explicit instead implicit. preset table exposed list_annotation_presets() also documents recommended input scale, symbol priority, fallback order, bundled truth resources lab can standardize annotation, also validation examples.

title: Theory and Design
text: expranno keeps two complementary representations: wide annotated matrix file export long merged table downstream analysis wide representation best exchange reproducibility: expr_anno.csv long representation best plotting, grouped summaries, sample-level joins: expr_meta_merged.csv Bioconductor-first projects, package also now provides as_expranno_input() SummarizedExperiment can converted strict contract without manual reshaping. reverse direction now supported . as_expranno_se() converts expranno_result expranno_annotation SummarizedExperiment : annotated expression main assay annotation fields rowData sample metadata colData optional signature deconvolution outputs appended sample-level colData columns

title: Theory and Design
text: Many downstream methods operate gene symbols rather Ensembl IDs, duplicated symbols collapsed annotation. expranno now exposes choice directly instead silently summing everything. count-like matrices, default "auto" rule uses "sum" abundance-like log-scale matrices, default "auto" rule uses "mean" matters summing TPM-like log-scale values usually good default. project needs different rule, strategy can set explicitly "sum", "mean", "max", "first".

title: Theory and Design
text: coverage-first design measurable, expranno now includes benchmark_annotation_engines(). function compares none, biomart, orgdb, ensdb, hybrid input reports: annotated gene counts per-field coverage ambiguity burden package’s main answer question, “hybrid engine actually improve annotation dataset?”

title: Theory and Design
text: Coverage necessary sufficient. expranno now also includes validate_annotation_engines(), compares chosen fields truth table keyed Ensembl gene ID. supports second question: recovered labels correct genes already trust?

title: Theory and Design
text: annotation merging, package supports: immune deconvolution immunedeconv pathway signature scoring GSVA ssGSEA designed write explicit CSV outputs results easy inspect outside R. deconvolution, expranno forwards method-specific arguments immunedeconv. important methods timer consensus_tme, require indications vector. signature scoring, expranno now uses current GSVA parameter-object workflow (gsvaParam() / ssgseaParam()) API available, keeping legacy fallback older GSVA versions. full wrapper also writes session_info.txt package versions R runtime used result directory can recovered later.

text: expr Expression table gene_id first column, SummarizedExperiment-like object. meta Metadata table sample first column. Leave NULL expr SummarizedExperiment metadata taken colData. species Either "auto", "human", "mouse". annotation_preset Optional preset fixes reproducible annotation configuration. Supported values "human_v102", "mouse_v102", "human_tpm_v102", "mouse_tpm_v102", "human_count_v102", "mouse_count_v102". annotation_engine Annotation backend strategy. Use "hybrid" try biomaRt, orgdb, EnsDb sequence. fields Optional annotation fields keep. strip_version Whether remove Ensembl version suffixes. biomart_version Fixed Ensembl release used biomaRt backend. biomart_host Optional explicit Ensembl host. biomart_mirror Optional Ensembl mirror name. assay_name Optional assay name expr SummarizedExperiment. gene_id_col Optional row-data column containing Ensembl IDs expr SummarizedExperiment. sample_col Metadata column use sample expr SummarizedExperiment. output_file Optional CSV path annotated matrix. report_file Optional CSV path coverage report. ambiguity_file Optional CSV path ambiguity report. provenance_file Optional CSV path backend provenance. verbose Whether emit progress messages.

title: expranno 2.4.0
text: Added CI-level release metadata checks DESCRIPTION, NEWS.md, CITATION.cff, inst/CITATION, README tarball example stay aligned. Added required optional-backend-core GitHub Actions job installs stable optional annotation signature backends main runs corresponding tests. Split optional backend installation core full profiles stable Bioconductor-backed checks can run every push heavier deconvolution stack remains available dedicated smoke workflow.

text: Fixed pkgdown GitHub Actions workflow allowing pkgdown install local package building reference examples.

title: expranno 2.3.0
text: Synchronized public version across DESCRIPTION, NEWS.md, README.md, citations, next pkgdown deployment. Slimmed repository dropping generated docs/ main relying pkgdown workflow rebuild publish site. Removed redundant figure assets simplified pkgdown workflow source tree stays smaller easier maintain.

title: expranno 2.2.2
text: Installed SummarizedExperiment, S4Vectors, SingleCellExperiment GitHub Actions Bioconductor-output tests vignettes pass consistently across CI matrix.

title: expranno 2.2.1
text: Guarded Bioconductor-output vignette example getting-started.Rmd pkgdown, R CMD check, release builds longer fail SummarizedExperiment installed.

title: expranno 2.2.0
text: Added example_annotation_truth() plus bundled human mouse truth tables validation examples longer depend ad hoc local files. Added as_expranno_se() convert annotated outputs back SummarizedExperiment annotation rowData sample-level outputs colData. Expanded list_annotation_presets() recommended input scale, backend cascade, symbol priority, bundled validation resource columns. Added preset reference vignette surfaced preset/truth/Bioconductor helpers clearly README pkgdown site. Added bundled optional-backend installation helper Docker recipe reproducible local backend validation. Strengthened public-package metadata CI coverage release workflows cross-platform checks.

title: expranno 2.1.0
text: Reworked hybrid annotation around fixed biomaRt release (Ensembl v102) human/mouse-specific symbol recovery, backend provenance capture, ambiguity reporting. Added explicit human mouse annotation presets human_tpm_v102 mouse_tpm_v102 repeatable lab workflows. Added annotation_report.csv, annotation_ambiguity.csv, annotation_provenance.csv, optional session_info.txt outputs main wrapper. Added benchmark_annotation_engines() none, biomart, orgdb, ensdb, hybrid can compared input. Added validate_annotation_engines() plus validation CSV outputs annotation can checked truth tables rather coverage alone. Added SummarizedExperiment / SingleCellExperiment input coercion as_expranno_input(). Added dedicated GitHub Actions workflow optional-backend smoke tests. Improved signature scoring failures empty annotation columns now raise clear error instead bubbling rows aggregate, optional smoke runner skips signature scoring symbols annotated.

title: expranno 2.0.2
text: Fixed EnsDb fallback annotation switching AnnotationFilter::GeneIdFilter(). Fixed automatic deconvolution method discovery current immunedeconv exports. Added skip-aware optional-backend smoke tests hybrid annotation, real-data-like deconvolution, end--end signature scoring. Added inst/scripts/run_smoke_optional_backends.R local backend verification runs write smoke-test outputs disk.

title: expranno 2.0.1
text: Switched signature scoring current GSVA parameter-object API available, legacy fallback older GSVA releases. Added expr_scale duplicate_strategy controls duplicate symbols longer silently summed expression types. Added deconv_args support main wrapper explicit handling indication-specific immunedeconv methods timer consensus_tme. Expanded tests documentation around duplicate handling, deconvolution method requirements, signature-scoring kernel choices.

title: expranno 2.00
text: Prepared GitHub-facing README, GitHub Actions, pkgdown deployment public dai540/expranno repository. Redesigned website workflow figure cleaner mermaid-based layout separates inputs, core processing, outputs, downstream analyses. Added package logo assets README pkgdown site. Expanded README getting-started guide clearer workflow framing, output interpretation, post-run quality checks. Kept human mouse case studies centered annotation coverage, merged outputs, downstream deconvolution/signature workflows.

title: expranno 1.00
text: First public release candidate. Added hybrid human mouse annotation workflow biomaRt, org.*.eg.db, optional EnsDb fallback. Added expression metadata integration helpers. Added immune deconvolution wrappers around immunedeconv. Added GSVA ssGSEA wrappers user-supplied gene set databases.
